query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Disregard', 'To ignore or not allow something to influence you', 'Critics argued that the plan might disregard the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disreputable', 'Not respectable, lacking repute, discreditable', 'The passage presents a disreputable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disrepute', 'A loss or lack of credit or repute', 'The essay highlights the disrepute of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disrobe', 'To undress someone or something', 'The author hoped the new policy would disrobe tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disrupt', 'To throw into confusion or disorder', 'Critics argued that the plan might disrupt the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissatisfy', 'To fail to satisfy', 'Critics argued that the plan might dissatisfy the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissect', 'To cut apart (something) to study its anatomy', 'The author hoped the new policy would dissect tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissection', 'The act of dissecting, or something dissected', 'Scholars noted a striking dissection between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissemble', 'Hide one''s true motives or feelings', 'The essay highlights the dissemble of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disseminate', 'To spread widely', '“The internet helps disseminate information quickly.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissension', 'An act of expressing dissent, especially spoken', 'The essay highlights the dissension of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissent', 'To differ in opinion', 'Over time, the community learned to dissent its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissentient', 'In opposition to a majority or official opinion', 'Over time, the community learned to dissentient its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissentious', 'Not in agreement or harmony', 'The passage presents a dissentious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissertation', 'A written essay, or thesis, especially for a doctoral degree', 'Scholars noted a striking dissertation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disservice', 'An ill turn or injury', 'Scholars noted a striking disservice between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissever', 'To separate, split apart', 'Critics argued that the plan might dissever the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissidence', 'Protest against official policy', 'Scholars noted a striking dissidence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissident', 'A person who opposes official policy', 'Her dissident became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissimilar', 'Not alike; different', '“Their viewpoints are completely dissimilar.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissimulate', 'To disguise or conceal under a false appearance', 'The author hoped the new policy would dissimulate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissipate', 'To drive away, disperse', 'The author hoped the new policy would dissipate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissipation', 'Squandering of money, energy, or resources', 'The essay highlights the dissipation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissolute', 'Lax in morals, licentious', 'The essay highlights the dissolute of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissolution', 'The termination of an organized body or legislative assembly', 'Scholars noted a striking dissolution between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissolve', 'To make a solution of, by mixing with a liquid', 'Critics argued that the plan might dissolve the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissonance', 'Inharmonious or harsh sound, cacophany', 'Her dissonance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissonant', 'Unsuitable in combination', 'The essay highlights the dissonant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissuade', 'To convince not to try or do', 'The author hoped the new policy would dissuade tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dissuasion', 'The act or an instance of persuading against', 'The essay highlights the dissuasion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distemper', 'To disturb and disorder, hence to make sick', 'Over time, the community learned to distemper its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distend', 'To extend or expand, as from internal pressure, to swell', 'Over time, the community learned to distend its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distensible', 'Capable of swelling or stretching', 'Her distensible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distention', 'The state of being stretched beyond normal dimensions', 'The essay highlights the distention of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distill', 'To make something (esp. a liquid) in this way', 'Over time, the community learned to distill its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distillate', 'Something formed by distilling', 'Scholars noted a striking distillate between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distillation', 'The act of pouring out or falling in drops', 'Scholars noted a striking distillation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distiller', 'A person who owns, works in or operates a distillery', 'Her distiller became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distinction', 'A difference or contrast between similar things or people', 'Her distinction became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distinctive', 'Having a unique or distinguishing quality', '“His distinctive voice made him a recognizable singer.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distinguish', 'To see someone or something as different from others', 'The author hoped the new policy would distinguish tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distort', 'Change something from its true shape or meaning', 'Her distort became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distrain', 'To squeeze, press, to constrain, oppress', 'Critics argued that the plan might distrain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distrainor', 'One who distrains, the party distraining goods or chattels', 'Scholars noted a striking distrainor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distraught', 'Deeply upset and agitated', 'Scholars noted a striking distraught between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Distrust', 'To put no trust in, to have no confidence in', 'The author hoped the new policy would distrust tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disunion', 'Separation of a union', 'Her disunion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Disyllable', 'A word comprising two syllables', 'Scholars noted a striking disyllable between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dither', 'To be uncertain or unable to make a decision about something', 'Critics argued that the plan might dither the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Diurnal', 'Happening during daylight, or active during that time', 'Scholars noted a striking diurnal between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
